translated: Here is the saying of Noowa: "Those words from people's mouths, when they are strong with the power of horns, so that they are good and their own home is secure." Noowa is the one who speaks this.

text: Ní Unimbɔti náań unil ki nántiǹ u-ba, u náań-u ki nántiǹ úǹ Unimbɔti u-ba ní. U nìn náań uninja nì unimpu ní.
translated: At Unimbɔti, we have no people who live there, but we have people who live outside Unimbɔti. And our children are in the fields there.

ref: Take into the boat with you a male and a female of every kind of animal and bird, as well as a male and a female of every reptile. I don't want them to be destroyed.
text: Á dāá lēē unoon kamaa buluku uja nì usal, nì kuwaŋkuluu kamaa buluku uja nì usal, nì kun kamaa pée fù kitiŋ puee buluku uja nì usal, bi-kɔkɔ ń dāá dāan̄ a-cee kí ŋmátí.
translated: Speak to the people, those who do work well, the leaders who do work well, the youth who do minor work well with strength—both say that it is important.

ref: After saying this, he rushed off to his herd of cattle and picked out one of the best calves, which his servant quickly prepared.
text: Abraam tùkū u-nimpuu mimmee, ní ki sáǹ ki cūtìi kɔ́ u-nacaŋkpuŋkun, ki cúú dinajapɔmbil din bí màlmalee ki tīī u-tɔntɔnliu ní úǹ kūū-dì ki ŋɔ́fì malaa malaa.
translated: Abraham said he heard me, and that the good work he cultivated, which the people have much of, when he prayed that he might not perish, give plenty of food repeatedly.

ref: When the stronger sheep were mating near the drinking place, Jacob made sure that the spotted branches were there.
text: Ŋyunti ŋun ní tiwaŋkuti tin yáàyee yaa làá jɔ̄m̀ tɔbee, ní Jaakɔb ń yòon̄ní ndɔsɔmu mun u cùkūee kí ŋá tiwan tin poon tiwaŋkuti nyùee kí túĺ ti-nimbiin, ní tì jɔ́ḿ tɔb ndɔsɔmu mun u cùkūee cee.
translated: People say that those who wander seek pasture without resting, and Jacob was watching his flock that he tends, so that they might find pasture and water for the livestock to graze, and when evening comes he brings back his flock that he tends.

ref: When Jacob saw them, he said, “This is God's camp.” So he named the place Mahanaim.
text: Ní Jaakɔb mun nín nìkì u-saŋŋu. U cée ní Unimbɔti tuuŋyi cílìń u-pu nsanni.
translated: Jacob is in his house. He says that in Unimbɔti, the people live in peace.

ref: So God created humans to be like himself; he made men and women.
text: Ní Unimbɔti náań unil ki nántiǹ u-ba, u náań-u ki nántiǹ úǹ Unimbɔti u-ba ní. U nìn náań uninja nì unimpu ní.
translated: In Unimbɔti, the people say that these come, and they say that these come from Unimbɔti here. And the people dwell in their villages here.

ref: Take into the boat with you a male and a female of every kind of animal and bird, as well as a male and a female of every reptile. I don't want them to be destroyed.
text: Á dāá lēē unoon kamaa buluku uja nì usal, nì kuwaŋkuluu kamaa buluku uja nì usal, nì kun kamaa pée fù kitiŋ puee buluku uja nì usal, bi-kɔkɔ ń dāá dāan̄ a-cee kí ŋmátí.
translated: Speak to the people, those who dwell in the village, the elders who dwell in the village, the youth who till the fields and dwell in the village—both of them declare this is important.

ref: After saying this, he rushed off to his herd of cattle and picked out one of the best calves, which his servant quickly prepared.
text: Abraam tùkū u-nimpuu mimmee, ní ki sáǹ ki cūtìi kɔ́ u-nacaŋkpuŋkun, ki cúú dinajapɔmbil din bí màlmalee ki tīī u-tɔntɔnliu ní úǹ kūū-dì ki ŋɔ́fì malaa malaa.
translated: Abraham said he declared to us that the way they proceed with his eternal covenant, and that those who serve among peaceful people, may live in their eternal home and have much abundance forever.

ref: When the stronger sheep were mating near the drinking place, Jacob made sure that the spotted branches were there.
text: Ŋyunti ŋun ní tiwaŋkuti tin yáàyee yaa làá jɔ̄m̀ tɔbee, ní Jaakɔb ń yòon̄ní ndɔsɔmu mun u cùkūee kí ŋá tiwan tin poon tiwaŋkuti nyùee kí túĺ ti-nimbiin, ní tì jɔ́ḿ tɔb ndɔsɔmu mun u cùkūee cee.
translated: People say that those who dance do not know the time of the journey, and Jacob is calling his people to gather so that we may hear the words of the speakers so that the truth may be known, and when the time of the journey comes for the people to gather, then.

ref: When Jacob saw them, he said, “This is God's camp.” So he named the place Mahanaim.
text: Ní Jaakɔb mun nín nìkì u-saŋŋu. U cée ní Unimbɔti tuuŋyi cílìń u-pu nsanni.
translated: Jacob is in the market selling. He says that in Unimbɔti, people buy his goods.

ref: So God created humans to be like himself; he made men and women.
text: Ní Unimbɔti náań unil ki nántiǹ u-ba, u náań-u ki nántiǹ úǹ Unimbɔti u-ba ní. U nìn náań uninja nì unimpu ní.
translated: In Unimbɔti, those people say that those ones come from Unimbɔti. They also say that those people live in the village here.

ref: Take into the boat with you a male and a female of every kind of animal and bird, as well as a male and a female of every reptile. I don't want them to be destroyed.
text: Á dāá lēē unoon kamaa buluku uja nì usal, nì kuwaŋkuluu kamaa buluku uja nì usal, nì kun kamaa pée fù kitiŋ puee buluku uja nì usal, bi-kɔkɔ ń dāá dāan̄ a-cee kí ŋmátí.
translated: O people, those who do daily work, the elders who do daily work, the youth who carry loads and do daily work, all together they declare that it is important.

ref: After saying this, he rushed off to his herd of cattle and picked out one of the best calves, which his servant quickly prepared.
text: Abraam tùkū u-nimpuu mimmee, ní ki sáǹ ki cūtìi kɔ́ u-nacaŋkpuŋkun, ki cúú dinajapɔmbil din bí màlmalee ki tīī u-tɔntɔnliu ní úǹ kūū-dì ki ŋɔ́fì malaa malaa.
translated: Abraham said, 'I heard that there is a path to the house I will build, that two people come together, and that when I worship with my strength, they will give many gifts.'

ref: When the stronger sheep were mating near the drinking place, Jacob made sure that the spotted branches were there.
text: Ŋyunti ŋun ní tiwaŋkuti tin yáàyee yaa làá jɔ̄m̀ tɔbee, ní Jaakɔb ń yòon̄ní ndɔsɔmu mun u cùkūee kí ŋá tiwan tin poon tiwaŋkuti nyùee kí túĺ ti-nimbiin, ní tì jɔ́ḿ tɔb ndɔsɔmu mun u cùkūee cee.
translated: People say that those who dance do not know the time of the journey, and Jacob is teaching the word to those who hear so that we may know the hearts of the listeners until the end of their lives, and indeed the time of the journey, the word to those who hear, is clear.

ref: When Jacob saw them, he said, “This is God's camp.” So he named the place Mahanaim.
text: Ní Jaakɔb mun nín nìkì u-saŋŋu. U cée ní Unimbɔti tuuŋyi cílìń u-pu nsanni.
translated: Jacob is the one who lives in the house. He says that in Unimbi, all the people do his work.

ref: So God created humans to be like himself; he made men and women.
text: Ní Unimbɔti náań unil ki nántiǹ u-ba, u náań-u ki nántiǹ úǹ Unimbɔti u-ba ní. U nìn náań uninja nì unimpu ní.
translated: In Unimbɔti, the people say that these come, and they say that these come from Unimbɔti here. They also do not know those languages here.

ref: Take into the boat with you a male and a female of every kind of animal and bird, as well as a male and a female of every reptile. I don't want them to be destroyed.
text: Á dāá lēē unoon kamaa buluku uja nì usal, nì kuwaŋkuluu kamaa buluku uja nì usal, nì kun kamaa pée fù kitiŋ puee buluku uja nì usal, bi-kɔkɔ ń dāá dāan̄ a-cee kí ŋmátí.
translated: I speak to the people—those who work every day, the elders who work every day, the youth who work with tools and every day—all together they declare that it is the truth.

ref: After saying this, he rushed off to his herd of cattle and picked out one of the best calves, which his servant quickly prepared.
text: Abraam tùkū u-nimpuu mimmee, ní ki sáǹ ki cūtìi kɔ́ u-nacaŋkpuŋkun, ki cúú dinajapɔmbil din bí màlmalee ki tīī u-tɔntɔnliu ní úǹ kūū-dì ki ŋɔ́fì malaa malaa.
translated: Abraham the man worships constantly, and whatever he declares to his God, he offers various foods and pure provisions, so that he may be heard from the heavens above, to protect his people abundantly.

ref: When the stronger sheep were mating near the drinking place, Jacob made sure that the spotted branches were there.
text: Ŋyunti ŋun ní tiwaŋkuti tin yáàyee yaa làá jɔ̄m̀ tɔbee, ní Jaakɔb ń yòon̄ní ndɔsɔmu mun u cùkūee kí ŋá tiwan tin poon tiwaŋkuti nyùee kí túĺ ti-nimbiin, ní tì jɔ́ḿ tɔb ndɔsɔmu mun u cùkūee cee.
translated: People say that workers cannot provide for themselves, and Jacob prays to our God to hear him so that we might be able to obtain enough for workers to sustain our lives, and indeed, only when our God hears him does he act thus.

ref: When Jacob saw them, he said, “This is God's camp.” So he named the place Mahanaim.
text: Ní Jaakɔb mun nín nìkì u-saŋŋu. U cée ní Unimbɔti tuuŋyi cílìń u-pu nsanni.
translated: Jacob is in his house. He says that in Unimbɔti, the people kill enemies.
